task_completion.description: At least two different types of simulated device adapters are running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenICE interface or terminal windows indicating that at least two distinct device adapters (e.g., Multiparameter Monitor, Infusion Pump, Pulse Oximeter, CO2/Capnograph, NIBP, Ventilator) have been started.

task_completion.description: At least two different clinical applications are evaluated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show at least two different OpenICE clinical applications (e.g., Vital Signs, Infusion Safety, Patient ID, Xray Viewer, Clinical Alarms) opened and visible in the GUI.

task_completion.description: A report file named clinical_app_evaluation.txt is created on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) is open editing a file named 'clinical_app_evaluation.txt', or the file is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The report includes the names of the evaluated applications and their clinical purposes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly names at least two OpenICE clinical applications and describes what they are used for clinically.

task_completion.description: The report includes a comparison of features and a deployment recommendation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report contains a section comparing the features of the evaluated applications and provides a clear recommendation regarding their hospital deployment.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the OpenICE GUI to evaluate the applications.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the OpenICE interface and viewing the apps, rather than just writing a report without opening the software.

integrity.description: The report was manually written or constructed in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing or editing the report in a text editor, rather than using a script or a single terminal command to dump pre-written text into the file.

integrity.description: The report content reflects actual features of the OpenICE applications.
integrity.visual_evidence: The details written in the report match the visual features seen in the OpenICE applications during the trajectory (e.g., mentioning waveforms for Vital Signs, or interlocks for Infusion Safety), rather than generic or hallucinated text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenICE simulated device types include: Multiparameter Monitor, Infusion Pump, Pulse Oximeter, CO2/Capnograph, NIBP, and Ventilator. OpenICE clinical applications include: Vital Signs, Infusion Safety, Patient ID, Xray Viewer, and Clinical Alarms. The report must be located at /home/ga/Desktop/clinical_app_evaluation.txt and should contain clinical terminology such as monitoring, safety, patient, thresholds, waveforms, or interlock.
